seo.homeTitle: Swiss public APIs — MCP server & CLI
seo.homeDesc: Two ways to use the same Swiss data tools: an MCP server for AI assistants (Claude, Cursor, …) and a standalone terminal CLI. Transport, geography, health, politics, business, energy, weather, food and statistics.
seo.toolsDesc: Browse every tool by category. The same names work in the MCP server and in the swiss-apis-cli package.
seo.installTitle: Install MCP or CLI — Swiss APIs
seo.installDesc: Install the MCP server in your AI client, or the CLI globally with npm — independently. Step-by-step for Claude, Cursor, Windsurf and Continue.dev.

hero.title2: in your AI or your terminal
hero.desc: Use {count} Swiss public-data tools either through an MCP-compatible assistant (swiss-apis-mcp) or from the command line (swiss-apis-cli). Same official APIs — pick the workflow that fits you.
hero.codeLabelMcp: MCP — add to your client config

mcp.title: The open standard for AI tool access
mcp.p1: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard developed by Anthropic that lets AI assistants — like Claude, Cursor or Windsurf — securely call external tools and access real-world data sources. Think of it as a universal plugin system for AI.
mcp.p2: swiss-apis-mcp is an MCP server: it runs locally on your machine, exposes {count} Swiss data tools, and any compatible AI client can call them naturally in conversation — no manual API calls, no code.
mcp.localDesc: The server runs on your machine via stdio. No data leaves your system except to the target Swiss APIs.
mcp.freeDesc: {free} out of {total} tools work without any API key. Just install and go.
mcp.officialDesc: All data comes from official Swiss government APIs — opendata.swiss, swisstopo, transport.opendata.ch and more.

categories.title: Everything Swiss — MCP or CLI
categories.desc: From real-time train departures to federal legislation, from company registry lookups to avalanche warnings — use the same tools in chat (MCP) or in scripts and terminals (CLI).

clients.title: Works with your favourite AI client
clients.desc: Any client supporting the Model Context Protocol can use swiss-apis-mcp. One config, four popular clients supported out of the box. (The CLI does not require an AI client.)

cta.title: Ready to use Swiss data?
cta.desc: Install the MCP server for your AI, or the CLI for your terminal — or both. Most tools need no API key. Open source, MIT licensed.

footer.desc: MCP server and CLI exposing {count} tools for Switzerland's official public APIs. Free and open source.
footer.legal: Data from official Swiss public APIs. Not affiliated with the Swiss Confederation.

tools.title: All {count} tools
tools.desc: Browse, search and filter every tool. Names match the MCP server and the swiss command-line client.
tools.mcpCliNote: Each tool is available via the MCP server (in compatible AI apps) and via the CLI with swiss call and a JSON payload. Install the two packages independently — see the installation page.
tools.emptyDesc: Try adjusting your search or clearing the category filter.
tools.ctaDesc: Follow the install guide for the MCP server and/or the CLI.

install.title: MCP server or CLI — your choice
install.desc: Install only what you need. The MCP server plugs into Claude, Cursor, Windsurf and similar clients. The CLI is a standalone npm package for terminals and scripts. Both call the same Swiss APIs; optional API keys work the same way, but key files are stored in different paths.
install.compareTitle: Two ways to access the same tools
install.compareIntro: Pick one or use both. They do not depend on each other.
install.cardMcpDesc: For AI assistants that support Model Context Protocol. You add a small JSON config; the client launches the server. Tools are invoked in natural language during chat.
install.cardCliDesc: For developers and terminals. Install the swiss command globally, then run swiss list, swiss call, swiss doctor. Ideal for scripts, CI and SSH sessions.
install.keysNoteBody: Environment variable names are the same for both (e.g. OTD_API_KEY, ZEFIX_USER / ZEFIX_PASS, FOODREPO_API_KEY). MCP stores keys in ~/.swiss-apis-mcp/keys; the CLI uses ~/.swiss-apis-cli/keys. You can also use a .env file in your working directory for the CLI.
install.prereq1Desc: Required for npx / npm and for running either package.
install.prereq2Title: For MCP only: a compatible AI client
install.prereq2Desc: Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf or Continue.dev — not needed for the CLI.
install.cliDesc: Install the package globally, then check that the swiss command works.
install.cliStep1Desc: Installs the swiss command on your PATH.
install.cliStep2Desc: Lists optional keys and whether they are set (nothing secret is printed).
install.cliReadmeHint: Full usage (JSON args, stdin, .env) is in the package README on npm.
install.step1Desc: You can skip this — npx -y in the config downloads the latest version. For a fixed, offline-capable MCP install:
install.step2Desc: Add the MCP server to your client's configuration file. Select your client below:
install.step3Desc: Once configured, ask your AI to run the built-in setup check:
install.step3Sub: The tool will list all {count} tools and indicate which optional API keys are configured. You can start using the free tools immediately — no key needed.
install.restartNote: After saving the config, restart your AI client to load the MCP server.
install.verifyPrompt: Run swiss_check_setup to see which Swiss API tools are available.
install.apiKeysDesc: Most tools ({free}/{total}) work without any API key. The following optional keys unlock additional tools. All are available for free — register on the respective portal.
install.apiKeyNote: MCP: use the swiss_set_api_key tool or ~/.swiss-apis-mcp/keys. CLI: use swiss_set_api_key or ~/.swiss-apis-cli/keys. Environment variables work for both.
install.examplesDesc: With the MCP server, ask your AI naturally. Examples:
install.ex1User: What are the next departures from Zurich HB to Bern in the next 30 minutes?
install.ex1Ai: Let me check the departures using the Swiss transport API… [calls swiss_transport_connections]
install.ex2User: What is the elevation of the Matterhorn? And which canton is it in?
install.ex2Ai: I'll use the swisstopo tools to find the exact elevation and identify the canton… [calls swiss_geo_get_elevation + swiss_geo_identify_canton]
install.ex3User: Find all active companies in Zurich named "Müller".
install.ex3Ai: Searching the ZEFIX company registry… [calls swiss_company_search]
install.ex4User: Search Swiss federal law for regulations about data protection.
install.ex4Ai: Searching Fedlex for relevant legislation… [calls swiss_federal_law_search]
install.sidebarStep1: Add the config snippet to your client
install.sidebarNote: No account needed. 30 of 34 tools work without an API key.

catDesc.setup: Check configuration status and store optional API keys — no server restart needed.
catDesc.transport: Real-time departures, connections, station search and GTFS data across Switzerland.
catDesc.geo: Elevation, canton/municipality identification, coordinate conversion and official swisstopo map layers.
catDesc.health: Federal Office of Public Health (BAG) datasets and 100+ health indicators by canton.
catDesc.politics: Parliamentary motions, federal legislation via Fedlex and councillors across 74+ parliaments.
catDesc.business: Company registry (ZEFIX), Official Gazette (SOGC) and 14,000+ open government datasets.
catDesc.energy: Swiss Federal Office of Energy (SFOE) datasets and Swissgrid electricity grid data.
catDesc.weather: MeteoSwiss open datasets, SLF avalanche warnings and BAFU hydrological data.
catDesc.food: Swiss food product database (FoodRepo) and official FSVO nutrition composition data.
catDesc.statistics: BFS/OFS statistical datasets, SNB financial data, postal codes and migration statistics.

apiKey.otdDesc: Required for real-time GTFS data (trip updates, vehicle positions, alerts).
apiKey.zefixDesc: Required to search the Swiss company registry (ZEFIX). Free registration on the official portal.
apiKey.foodrepoDesc: Required to search Swiss food products by name or barcode.
